parts.text: Outline reasons why farmers install cow brushes in cattle sheds. Allow cows to groom or scratch / reduce skin irritations / reduce stress or improved animal welfare / encourage natural behaviour / cleaner cows or improved hygiene
solution: Allow cows to groom or scratch
Reduce stress or improved animal welfare

parts.text: Describe the advantages of one other named piece of farm technology in relation to environmental sustainability. 3+1 Two points on any named valid piece of technology relating to environmental sustainability OR (b)(i) Briefly explain how this technology would be safer for (1) animals and (2) the environment. Animals: Less risk to animals from noxious (harmful) gases 4+1 Environment: Reduction of greenhouse gases (which contribute to air pollution) / reduced runoff due to increased nutrient absorption by plants
solution: Two points on any named valid piece of technology relating to environmental sustainability
Briefly explain how this technology would be safer for (1) animals and (2) the environment. Animals: Less risk to animals from noxious (harmful) gases
Environment: Reduction of greenhouse gases (which contribute to air pollution) / reduced runoff due to increased nutrient absorption by plants

subparts.text: Briefly explain how this technology would be safer for (1) animals and (2) the environment. Animals: Less risk to animals from noxious (harmful) gases 4+1 Environment: Reduction of greenhouse gases (which contribute to air pollution) / reduced runoff due to increased nutrient absorption by plants
solution: Less risk to animals from noxious (harmful) gases
Reduction of greenhouse gases (which contribute to air pollution)
Reduced runoff due to increased nutrient absorption by plants

parts.text: Outline the benefits of the nitrogen cycle to animal and/or crop production on Irish farms. Symbiotic bacteria or Rhizobium can convert N compounds into nitrates (nitrification). Legumes (clover) carry out nitrogen fixation. Nitrates are available for plant use. Provides N to build proteins in plants or animals. Healthy plant growth. Less fertiliser needed. More productive swards (so less need for concentrates). More efficient fertiliser or slurry use. Soil fertility maintenance.
solution: Symbiotic bacteria or Rhizobium can convert N compounds into nitrates (nitrification). Legumes (clover) carry out nitrogen fixation. Nitrates are available for plant use. Provides N to build proteins in plants or animals. Healthy plant growth. Less fertiliser needed. More productive swards (so less need for concentrates). More efficient fertiliser or slurry use. Soil fertility maintenance.

parts.text: Identify both cattle breeds in A and B. A: Aberdeen Angus B: Hereford
State which breed A or B has a dominant polled trait as a characteristic of the breed.
Explain the underlined term in part (b) above. Does not have horns

Outline ways farmers can ensure quality produced on their farms to ensure they meet these standards for the export markets. Be part of quality assurance scheme / maintain hygiene to reduce disease or contamination / implement biosecurity measures / use tagging or animal passports (for traceability of produce) / suitable health and welfare measures taken with housing or disease prevention / good quality feed for livestock
solution: Participate in a quality assurance scheme
Maintain hygiene to reduce disease or contamination
Use tagging or animal passports (for traceability of produce)
Implement suitable health and welfare measures with housing or disease prevention
Provide good quality feed for livestock

parts.text: Briefly explain the importance of export markets to Irish agriculture. Contribute to farm income / surplus produce can be exported / 4 economic or employment benefit to country
solution: Export markets contribute to farm income
Export markets provide 4 economic benefits to the country
Export markets contribute to employment benefits

context: Outline one scientific reason for any four.

parts.text: Outline one scientific reason for any four.
Having a buffer zone close to a waterway.
Presence of chloroplasts in the palisade layer of a leaf.
solution: Close to surface to maximise photosynthesis

parts.text: Culling of breeding ewes on a sheep farm.
Movement of water from the soil through the plant to the atmosphere.
